stub: He is a Conservative MP who comes from a white working class background.
constituency: He is from a constituency that is not your constituency.
personalization: He tends to vote in accordance with the instructions of his party, even when that conflicts with the wishes of his constituents.
substantive: He supports increasing the basic Universal Credit grant by £20 per week.
justification: In political debates, he regularly explains how his policies matter to people like you (e.g. of your class and educational background).
responsiveness: Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, he said: “I went into politics to implement good ideas, not popular ones that win elections.”
text: He is a Conservative MP who comes from a white working class background. He is from a constituency that is not your constituency. He tends to vote in accordance with the instructions of his party, even when that conflicts with the wishes of his constituents. He supports increasing the basic Universal Credit grant by £20 per week. In political debates, he regularly explains how his policies matter to people like you (e.g. of your class and educational background). Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, he said: “I went into politics to implement good ideas, not popular ones that win elections.”
